claimText: Asian Elephants have a matriarchal social structure and a 22-month gestation period, which is the longest among mammals.
evidenceExcerpt: Asian Elephants have a matriarchal social structure and the longest gestation period in mammals at 22 months.
examUse: Statement-based question on elephant biology and social behavior.
distractorHooks: Shorter gestation period such as 12 or 18 months

claimText: The conservation status of Asian Elephants is Endangered under the IUCN Red List, Schedule I under the WPA 1972, and Appendix I under CITES.
evidenceExcerpt: Conservation Status of Asian Elephants: IUCN Endangered, WPA 1972 Schedule I, and CITES Appendix I.
examUse: Correct-pair or statement-based question on species conservation status.
distractorHooks: Vulnerable or Critically Endangered IUCN status

claimText: Project Elephant was launched in 1992 and currently comprises 33 Elephant Reserves across 14 major states.
evidenceExcerpt: Project Elephant was launched in 1992, and there are currently 33 Elephant Reserves across 14 major states.
examUse: Statement-based question on Indian conservation initiatives.
distractorHooks: Different number of reserves or states

claimText: Asian elephants possess a twin-domed head and a humped or convex back, whereas African elephants have a single-domed head and a concave or dipped back.
evidenceExcerpt: Asian elephants have a twin-domed head with a central indent and a humped/convex back, whereas African elephants have a rounded/flat single dome and a concave/dipped back.
examUse: Comparison question distinguishing physical traits of Asian and African elephants.
distractorHooks: Asian elephants have a concave back
African elephants have a twin-domed head

claimText: Only some male Asian elephants have visible tusks, whereas both male and female African elephants have larger tusks.
evidenceExcerpt: Only some male Asian elephants have visible tusks, while both male and female African elephants have larger tusks.
examUse: Statement-based question on physical characteristics and sexual dimorphism.
distractorHooks: Both male and female Asian elephants have tusks
Only male African elephants have tusks
